meta.note: Derived compute characterization; case trees are not committed.

machine.solver: interFoam (VOF), OpenFOAM ESI v2312 — single-threaded per case

parallel_efficiency.wallclock_speedup_x: ~7-10 (range: serial baseline 247-336 min depending on sim-time scaling)
parallel_efficiency.note: 14-wide fan-out finished the batch ~7-10x faster in wall-clock than one-at-a-time (serial baseline 247-336 min), despite each case running ~2.3x slower under memory-bandwidth contention — net throughput win.

runtime_model.explanation: Wall time scales with the product of mesh cells and solver timesteps; the mesh-normalised cost (µs per cell·timestep) is the machine invariant, and f(concurrency) is the contention factor for parallel fan-out.

intrinsic_cost_us_per_cell_timestep: ~12-17 (static free-decay ~12; single uncontended moving-mesh forced ~16-17). Per-class figures below are contention-inflated (~1.5-2.3x).

analysis.headline: This box runs 2D interFoam sloshing at an intrinsic ~12-17 us per cell·timestep (static free-decay ~12; a single uncontended moving-mesh forced case ~16-17). The 22-28 us per-class figures are NOT a physics cost curve — they are the same work inflated ~1.5-2.3x by memory-bandwidth contention when 14-16 cases share the 32-core / 31 GB box. Contention, not mesh motion, is the dominant compute-planning lever; a 14-wide fan-out still cut the 48-case backbone from ~5-6 h serial to 34.7 min (~7-10x wall-clock).

projections.tier: Courant / mesh convergence — free-decay 160×160, Co halved
projections.assumptions: static-mesh @ ~12 us/cell·ts intrinsic; 25,600 cells × ~12,000 timesteps; ~4 cases fit one fan-out wave
3,600 cells × ~7,000 ts; 2 fan-out waves of 14; +10-20% if impacts cut dt

projections.tier: 3D L-shaped ballast tank — extrude ~60-100 cells in 3rd dim
projections.assumptions: 216k-360k cells × ~7,000 ts, moving mesh; 3D cost/cell an unmeasured ~1.2× extrapolation; single-thread per case
projections.est_wall: ~12-24 h single-thread per case; ~1-2.5 h with ~16-core MPI (UNMEASURED — benchmark first)

caveats: Contention is memory-bandwidth-bound (not compute-bound): 14-16 concurrent interFoam on 32 cores / 31 GB slow each ~2.3x; the 22-28 us/cell·ts figures are batch-specific artifacts, not physics.
No large intrinsic moving-mesh premium: the raw 12→22→28 ordering conflates mesh motion with contention; use ~12-17 us intrinsic for 2D planning.
Adaptive-timestep variance: forced/backbone timesteps vary ±10-20% case-to-case with the free-surface dynamics; runtimes are medians, not guarantees.
2D→3D is an unmeasured extrapolation (assumed 1.2× 3D cost/cell); the ~1-2.5 h MPI figure must be benchmarked on this host before it is promised.
Fan-out speedup is a ~7-10x range: the serial baseline is 247 min (measured 309 s/case) to 336 min (sim-time-scaled); sub-14 case counts finish in a single wave.
